title: CleanSpark Expands Beyond Bitcoin Mining into AI Data Centers
text: CleanSpark, Inc. (Nasdaq: CLSK) announced a significant strategic pivot on October 20, 2025, transitioning from a pure-play bitcoin mining operation to include AI compute infrastructure development. The company appointed Jeffrey Thomas as Senior Vice President of AI Data Centers. Thomas brings over 40 years of experience in emerging technologies and data center infrastructure. In his previous role at Humain, he led the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's multi-billion AI data center program, assembling partnerships with major technology firms. CEO Matt Schultz stated that Thomas's decades of experience building and scaling digital infrastructure platforms will be instrumental as the company expands operations. The move aims to diversify revenue streams, strengthen cash flow, and serve leading technology companies demanding AI infrastructure. Chief Development Officer Scott Garrison highlighted Georgia as a strategic focus region. The company recently contracted for additional power and real estate in College Park to serve the Atlanta area. Management is evaluating expansion opportunities across its existing portfolio to meet significant customer demand for high-value compute capacity. CleanSpark leverages its proven expertise in large-scale bitcoin mining infrastructure to develop AI data centers, building on its established land and power assets. The expansion positions the company within the accelerating demand for digital infrastructure driven by AI and compute-intensive applications.
